tools.name: a11y_collapse
tools.description: Collapse a tree node, combo box, or disclosure element by accessibility name. Counterpart of a11y_expand.

tools.name: a11y_expand
tools.description: Expand a tree node, combo box, or disclosure element by accessibility name. Uses UIA ExpandCollapsePattern on Windows and AXExpanded attribute on macOS. More reliable than click-to-toggle when the element is already in a mixed state.

tools.name: a11y_get_element
tools.description: Fetch a single element by accessibility name — returns name, role, bounds, value, state flags (focused/enabled/disabled/selected/busy/offscreen/expandable/expanded). Convenience singular form of find_element (which returns an array).

tools.name: a11y_get_value
tools.description: Read the current value of a named text field, slider, or editable element (UIA ValuePattern / AX AXValue). Use this to verify what a form field contains before deciding whether to type into it.

tools.name: a11y_list_children
tools.description: List a11y elements geometrically contained within a named parent element. Useful for enumerating menu items, tree children, dialog buttons, list rows. Matches by bounds containment — an element is a child if its rect is fully inside the parent's rect. NOT a true a11y-tree child relationship; good enough for most UI patterns.

parentName.description: Accessibility name of the parent element

controlType.description: Optional role filter on the PARENT match

tools.name: a11y_select
tools.description: Select a list item, tab, or radio button by accessibility name. Uses UIA SelectionItemPattern on Windows; sets AXSelected on macOS. More reliable than clicking when an item is scrolled out of view.

tools.name: a11y_toggle
tools.description: Toggle a checkbox, switch, or toggle-button by accessibility name. Uses UIA TogglePattern on Windows and AXValue flip on macOS. Returns the NEW toggle state (On/Off/Indeterminate) in the result.

tools.name: cdp_click
tools.description: Click a DOM element by CSS selector or by visible text content.

text.description: Visible text of the element to click (alternative to selector)

tools.name: cdp_connect
tools.description: Connect to Edge/Chrome browser via Chrome DevTools Protocol (port 9223). Must be called before other cdp_* tools. Use navigate_browser to launch Edge with CDP enabled.

tools.name: cdp_evaluate
tools.description: Execute JavaScript in the browser page context. Returns the result.

javascript.description: JavaScript code to evaluate in the page

tools.name: cdp_list_tabs
tools.description: List all open browser tabs with their URLs and titles.

tools.name: cdp_page_context
tools.description: Get a structured list of interactive elements on the current browser page (inputs, buttons, links with selectors and positions).

tools.name: cdp_read_text
tools.description: Read text content from a DOM element. Useful for extracting information from web pages.

selector.description: CSS selector (default: "body" for full page text)

tools.name: cdp_scroll
tools.description: Scroll the browser page via DOM (window.scrollBy). Works regardless of mouse position — use for reliable page scrolling.

tools.name: cdp_select_option
tools.description: Select an option in a <select> dropdown by value or visible text.

value.description: Option value or visible text to select

tools.name: cdp_switch_tab
tools.description: Switch CDP connection to a different browser tab by URL or title substring.

target.description: URL or title substring to match

tools.name: cdp_type
tools.description: Type text into a DOM input field by CSS selector or by associated label text.

selector.description: CSS selector for the input field

label.description: Label text associated with the input (alternative to selector)

text.description: Text to type into the field

tools.name: cdp_wait_for_selector
tools.description: Wait for a DOM element matching a CSS selector to appear and become visible.

tools.name: close_window
tools.description: Polite close request — the app receives WM_CLOSE / AXCloseAction / _NET_CLOSE_WINDOW and may prompt ("Save changes?") or refuse. Returns when the request was posted, NOT when the window actually closed.

tools.name: delegate_to_agent
tools.description: Delegate a task to clawdcursor's autonomous pipeline (runs independently with its own LLM reasoning). Returns when the task completes or times out.

tools.name: desktop_screenshot
tools.description: Take a screenshot of the entire screen, resized to 1280px wide. Returns the image and scale metadata. Use read_screen (accessibility tree) first — only screenshot when you need visual confirmation.

tools.name: desktop_screenshot_region
tools.description: Take a zoomed screenshot of a specific screen region for detailed inspection. Coordinates are in image-space (from desktop_screenshot).

x.description: Left edge X in image-space coordinates

y.description: Top edge Y in image-space coordinates

tools.name: detect_webview_apps
tools.description: Enumerate running Electron / WebView2 / Chromium-shell apps (e.g. New Outlook, Teams, Discord, Slack, VS Code) whose accessibility trees are typically sparse because the UI is rendered inside a web view. Returns each candidate with a CDP-discovery hint — if a remote-debugging port is already live, you can attach to it via `browser({"action":"connect"})`; otherwise the hint shows the relaunch command the user should run to enable CDP. Use this WHEN `read_screen` returns an unexpectedly empty tree on a "native" app.

tools.name: find_element
tools.description: Search for UI elements by name, control type, or automation ID within a process. Returns matching elements with bounds.

tools.name: focus_element
tools.description: Put keyboard focus on a UI element by accessibility name. Does NOT raise the window — use focus_window first if you also need the window in the foreground. Returns not_supported_on_platform on Linux (AT-SPI bridge pending).

processId.description: Optional process id to scope the search

tools.name: focus_window
tools.description: Bring a window to the foreground. Matches by process name, PID, or title substring. Verifies focus after attempt.

tools.name: get_active_window
tools.description: Get the currently focused/foreground window.

tools.name: get_element_state
tools.description: Return only the state flags of a named element (focused, enabled/disabled, selected, busy, offscreen, expandable, expanded). Use for quick state checks without pulling the entire element record.

tools.name: get_focused_element
tools.description: Get the currently focused UI element (keyboard focus). Returns name, control type, value, bounds, and process ID.

tools.name: get_screen_size
tools.description: Get the screen dimensions and scale factor.

tools.name: get_system_time
tools.description: Return the current system time as ISO 8601 UTC + local components. Zero I/O.

tools.name: get_windows
tools.description: List all visible windows with their title, process name, PID, and bounds.

tools.name: invoke_element
tools.description: Invoke a UI Automation action on an element. More precise than smart_click — supports set-value, get-value, focus, expand, collapse in addition to click.

automationId.description: Element automation ID (more precise than name)

tools.name: key_down
tools.description: Press a key (or modifier) without releasing. Pair with key_up. Enables hold-modifier-while-click or chord-hold workflows. Accepts the same tokens as key_press ("shift", "Return", "F5", etc.).

tools.name: key_press
tools.description: Press a keyboard key or key combination. Use "+" for combos (e.g. "ctrl+s", "shift+enter", "alt+tab"). Single keys: "Return", "Tab", "Escape", "Backspace", "Delete", "F1"-"F12", "Left/Right/Up/Down".

tools.name: key_up
tools.description: Release a key previously pressed with key_down. No-op if not currently down.

tools.name: list_displays
tools.description: Enumerate all connected displays with logical bounds, physical size, DPI ratio, and primary flag. Use this before desktop_screenshot with displayIndex to target a specific monitor.

tools.name: maximize_window
tools.description: Maximize the foreground window (or a specific window matched by processName/title). Polite request — the OS window manager may snap to the full working area.

tools.name: minimize_window
tools.description: Minimize a window. Matches by process name, PID, or title. Cross-platform: Windows (ShowWindow), macOS (miniaturize), Linux (wmctrl/xdotool).

tools.name: minimize_window_to_taskbar
tools.description: Minimize a window (hide to taskbar/Dock). Counterpart to the existing `minimize_window` in a11y.ts but routed through the OS-agnostic PlatformAdapter and accepts explicit target queries.

tools.name: mouse_click
tools.description: Click the left mouse button at the given image-space coordinates.

tools.name: mouse_double_click
tools.description: Double-click the left mouse button at the given image-space coordinates.

tools.name: mouse_down
tools.description: Press a mouse button without releasing. Pair with mouse_up. Useful for hold-and-drag gestures, modifier-click selections, or chord input.

tools.name: mouse_drag
tools.description: Drag from one image-space coordinate to another (click-hold-move-release). Useful for selecting text, moving objects, or resizing.

tools.name: mouse_drag_stepped
tools.description: Drag the mouse along a multi-point path in image-space. Path is a JSON string of {x,y} points. Useful for Paint-style drawing or gesture input. Press occurs at the first point and release at the last.

path.description: JSON array of {"x":n, "y":n} points in image-space, min 2 points

tools.name: mouse_hover
tools.description: Move the mouse to the given image-space coordinates without clicking. Useful for revealing tooltips or hover menus.

tools.name: mouse_middle_click
tools.description: Middle-click (wheel-click) at image-space (x, y). Opens links in new tab in most browsers; pans in some apps.

tools.name: mouse_move_relative
tools.description: Move the cursor by a relative offset (dx, dy) in image-space pixels. Useful for drawing on a canvas or adjusting a selection. On Wayland where cursor-query APIs are blocked, uses the last known position.

tools.name: mouse_right_click
tools.description: Right-click at the given image-space coordinates (opens context menu).

tools.name: mouse_scroll
tools.description: Scroll the mouse wheel at the given image-space coordinates.

tools.name: mouse_scroll_horizontal
tools.description: Scroll horizontally at image-space (x, y). On Windows uses Shift+wheel synthesis; on Linux uses xdotool wheel buttons when available; on macOS uses Shift+wheel.

tools.name: mouse_triple_click
tools.description: Triple-click the left mouse button at image-space (x, y) — selects a paragraph in most text editors.

tools.name: mouse_up
tools.description: Release a mouse button previously pressed with mouse_down. No-op if nothing is held.

tools.name: navigate_browser
tools.description: Open a URL in the browser. Launches with CDP enabled (port 9223) for DOM interaction. Call cdp_connect after.

tools.name: ocr_read_screen
tools.description: Read all text on screen using OS-level OCR. Returns text elements with pixel coordinates (bounding boxes). Much cheaper than a screenshot — use this to find text, buttons, labels, and their positions. Coordinates are in real screen pixels.

tools.name: open_app
tools.description: Open an application by name. Uses platform-native launch.

tools.name: open_file
tools.description: Open a file or folder in the OS default application. Uses `open` (macOS), `xdg-open` (Linux), and explorer / ShellExecute (Windows).

tools.name: open_url
tools.description: Open a URL in the OS default browser. Non-browser-agnostic counterpart to navigate_browser.

tools.name: read_clipboard
tools.description: Read the current text content of the OS clipboard.

tools.name: read_screen
tools.description: Read the accessibility tree of the screen. Returns structured text showing: WINDOWS (all open windows), FOCUSED WINDOW UI TREE (buttons, inputs, text elements with coordinates), and FOCUSED ELEMENT (keyboard focus). This is fast, small, and structured — prefer this over screenshots.

processId.description: Focus on a specific process ID (optional — reads foreground window by default)

tools.name: relaunch_with_cdp
tools.description: Relaunch a WebView2 / Electron app with a remote-debugging port enabled so `browser` tools can attach. The relaunch closes the existing instance (asks the OS to close politely via window_close first), then starts a new one with `--remote-debugging-port=<port>`. The user MUST consent — this WILL lose any unsaved work in the app. Returns instructions + the new port. Prefer the hint from `detect_webview_apps` when possible.

port.description: Port to open for CDP. Default 9222.

tools.name: resize_window
tools.description: Set a window's logical-pixel bounds. Pass only the dimensions you want to change; omitted fields preserve the current value. Coordinates are logical pixels — top-left origin.

tools.name: restore_window
tools.description: Restore a minimized or maximized window back to its previous bounds. Use this to bring a hidden window back without toggling into fullscreen.

tools.name: shortcuts_execute
tools.description: Execute a keyboard shortcut by describing what you want to do (e.g. "scroll down", "close tab", "upvote"). Uses fuzzy matching against the shortcuts database. Provide context (active app name) for app-specific shortcuts like Reddit or Outlook.

intent.description: What you want to do (e.g. "scroll down", "new tab", "copy", "upvote", "reply to email")

context.description: Active app context for app-specific shortcuts (e.g. "reddit", "outlook"). Auto-detected from active window if omitted.

tools.name: shortcuts_list
tools.description: List available keyboard shortcuts. Filter by category (navigation, browser, editing, social, window, file, view, quick) and/or context (e.g. "reddit", "outlook"). Returns shortcut names, descriptions, and key combos for the current platform. Use this BEFORE reaching for mouse_scroll or mouse_click — there is often a faster keyboard shortcut.

category.description: Filter by category: navigation, browser, editing, social, window, file, view, quick. Omit to list all.

context.description: Filter by app context (e.g. "reddit", "outlook", "x.com"). Shows context-specific shortcuts that match.

tools.name: smart_click
tools.description: Click a UI element by name with automatic fallback. OCR-first: scans screen text and clicks by coordinates. Also tries a11y invoke (in parallel) and CDP as fallbacks. No screenshot or coordinate math needed — just provide the element text.

processId.description: Limit search to a specific process

tools.name: smart_read
tools.description: Read text from the screen with automatic fallback. OCR-first pipeline: runs OCR (primary) and a11y tree (supplement) in parallel. Returns structured text without needing a screenshot. Use this as your primary perception tool.

scope.description: Read scope: "focused" for focused element, "window" for active window, "screen" for full screen

target.description: Element name to read from specifically

tools.name: smart_type
tools.description: Type text into a UI element. If target is specified, finds and focuses the element first. Uses clipboard paste for reliability (no dropped characters).

target.description: Element name to focus before typing (optional — types into currently focused element if omitted)

processId.description: Limit search to a specific process

tools.name: switch_tab_os
tools.description: Cycle or jump to a browser tab using the OS-agnostic keyboard shortcut (mod+Tab / mod+Shift+Tab / mod+{1..9}). Works in Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari. For in-browser DOM-level control use cdp_switch_tab.

index.description: Jump to tab N (1-based, 1-9). If omitted, direction must be provided.

direction.description: Cycle "next" or "previous". Ignored when `index` is given.

tools.name: type_text
tools.description: Type text into the currently focused element via clipboard paste (reliable, no dropped chars).

tools.name: undo_last
tools.description: Emit the OS-specific Undo shortcut (Ctrl+Z on Win/Linux, Cmd+Z on macOS) into the focused window.

tools.name: wait
tools.description: Wait for a specified duration. Useful after animations or page loads.

tools.name: wait_for_element
tools.description: Poll the accessibility tree until an element appears or timeout elapses. Useful after an action that triggers a dialog or side panel. Returns not_supported_on_platform on Linux (AT-SPI bridge pending).

processId.description: Scope the search to a process

tools.name: write_clipboard
tools.description: Write text to the OS clipboard.
